comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (advisory=10, signed). Triage: (1) G1G2/G6/T3/T8 (5 findings) — the plan names only criteria_routing.json but validate-routing enforces a bijection with CANONICAL_LLM (registry.py:84); adding a routing key without adding it to CANONICAL_LLM => ORPHAN => validate-routing exit!=0. So registry.py IS an edit target. Criterion id = 'hedge' (matches the ticket's plan_review_hedge.md; criterion_prompt_id -> plan-review-hedge). Also regenerating the derived reviewers/index.json (drift gate). (2) T5e major — the exec:1-TURN-not-DET decision is load-bearing + non-obvious; recording it durably in ADR 0033 (not just the ticket). (3) E5 minor — adding an abstain-branch eval case for E4 scope-exclusion (external-fact exclusion -> abstain-with-coverage), not only a recall case. (4) COH/E3 — the title says 'exec:DET' but the body/decision is exec:1-TURN; implementing per the BODY (correct); ADR 0033 records the rejected exec:DET alternative. (5) depends_on WS1 already recorded (WS2 was blocked_by WS1, now closed).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Turn open-ended assumption checking into an enumerable probe-each scan, and add a cheap early hedge signal -- both feeding WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim sub-answer (introduced by WS1; this story depends_on WS1).

## What (decisions made -- no open forks)
- G-7(a): add a confident-assertion trigger lexicon to plan_review_E4.md as its SCAN PROTOCOL. Enumerate the lexicon IN the prompt: assertion frames like "X already {does/handles/returns/supports} Y", "X is {safe/idempotent/atomic}", "there are no X", "X guarantees Y". E4 already says "scan for assertions" -> this sharpens it.
- G-4: E4 scope-exclusion sub-check at NORMAL priority. Discrimination rule co-located in the rubric: FIRE on empirically-checkable (codebase) exclusions ("X already exists/handled in code"); ABSTAIN-with-coverage on external-fact exclusions ("another team owns X"). Add an AC for the abstain branch.
- G-7(b) HEDGE DETECTOR -- DECISION: implement as an exec:1-TURN finder criterion (matching E6's tier), NOT exec:DET. Rationale (verified): exec:DET cannot scan plan-prose -- engine_b.scan (det_invariants.py:112) operates on code-file ASTs/patterns; route_criteria (orchestrator.py:332) strips exec:DET criteria before pass1_chunk; partition_findings stamps DET results validity=1.0, bypassing Pass-2 grading. A 1-TURN finder flows through the EXISTING pass1_chunk -> verifier -> pass3 pipeline with ZERO new infrastructure and lets Pass-2 grade the hedge substance via committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim.
- DEDUP vs E6.no_hedges -- DECISION (verified: no location-based dedup exists; mint_finding_id hashes finding-text+criteria, not location): make dedup a RUBRIC-LEVEL rule inside plan_review_hedge.md, no pipeline change: "If the hedge sits in an AC clause (E6.no_hedges territory -- an AC using a hedge in place of a proving command), mark THIS criterion not-applicable and do not report it; E6 owns that case." This is testable with a FakeRunner case.

## What (edit targets)
plan_review_E4.md (lexicon + exclusion sub-check); a new plan_review_hedge.md prompt (the 1-TURN hedge finder, incl. the AC-clause suppression rule); criteria_routing.json (register the hedge criterion exec:1-TURN + E4 checklist entries).

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E4 confident-assertion lexicon enumerated in plan_review_E4.md; scope-exclusion sub-check present with the codebase-vs-external discrimination rule AND an abstain-with-coverage branch. Proof: `python -m rebar.llm.plan_review.registry validate-routing` exits 0; author recall case `test_e4_scope_exclusion` in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py and run `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k e4_scope_exclusion` green.
- [ ] Hedge detector registered as exec:1-TURN in criteria_routing.json with plan_review_hedge.md; flows through pass1_chunk->verifier->pass3 (no exec:DET). Proof: validate-routing exits 0; recall case `test_hedge_finder` green.
- [ ] Dedup is a RUBRIC-LEVEL suppression in plan_review_hedge.md (hedge inside an AC clause -> this criterion marks not-applicable; E6 reports it). Proof: FakeRunner case `test_hedge_dedup` where a hedge inside an AC clause is reported by E6 only, not the hedge finder; `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k hedge_dedup` green.
- [ ] depends_on WS1 recorded (committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim is introduced there).

file_impact.reason: NEW exec:1-TURN hedge finder prompt (NOT exec:DET -- DET cannot scan prose)
register hedge criterion exec:1-TURN + E4 checklist entries; dedup vs E6.no_hedges

compiled_state.title: WS2: E4 detectors + exec:DET hedge detector feeding the shared sub-answer
